title: syzygium quadratum
intro: Syzygium quadratum, also known as the Water Apple or Wild Apple, is a species of plant in the family Myrtaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark and leaves, which contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Syzygium quadratum include its potential to aid in reducing fever, alleviate cough, and provide relief from digestive issues. The plant's bark and leaves are often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies and folk medicine.
